text: A more probable reading in the Greek makes the distance not five furlongs, but about twenty miles, which would be a just estimate.

vv. 29-31. The situation is not made fully clear, either in the Greek text or in the Latin. There seems to be a gap between verses 30 and 31, perhaps due to a mistake in the manuscripts. The allusion to faults committed ‘through inadvertence’ is perhaps only a diplomatic formula for granting a general amnesty.
